# Extreme Precision

## Wafer Steppers

[How an ASML Lithography Machine Moves a Wafer](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1fOA85xtYxs)  

> The mythical zero-backlash drive exists here because the actuator (magnetic field) operates directly on a *floating* axis, no real transmission or structural element exists. 

![asml-litho](asml-litho.png)

![air-bearings-and-interferometers](litho-interferometers.png)

![metroframe](metroframe-etc.png)  
![bodes](asml-bodes.png)  

[Performance Results of a New Generation of 300mm Lithography Systems](performance-results-of-300mm-litho.pdf)  
[Control of Wafer Scanners: Methods and Developments](Control_of_Wafer_Scanners_Methods_and_Developments.pdf)  

- linear motors (long stroke) and voice coil actuators (short stroke)
- older generations were air-bearings and differential interferometers
- latest (in a vacuum) are maglev on hallbach arrays w/ many interferometers 

![maglev](maglev-stage.png)

## NIST MechE

![one](nist/nist-01.jpg)
![one](nist/nist-02.jpg)
![one](nist/nist-03.jpg)
![one](nist/nist-04.jpg)
![one](nist/nist-05.jpg)
![one](nist/nist-06.jpg)
![one](nist/nist-07.jpg)
![one](nist/nist-08.jpg)
